facts.description: Count of June 2026 customer-facing resolved ticket rows after all exclusions.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if the infographic communicates that 18 tickets/cases are counted after filtering. It may appear as denominator, KPI, or text; wording such as valid tickets, counted tickets, or resolved customer tickets is equivalent.

facts.description: Share of valid tickets where first_response_min is within the plan-specific SLA threshold.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if first-response SLA performance is shown as about 72%, 72.2%, or 13/18. It can be a KPI, label, fraction, or sentence; exact hyphenation/case of SLA is irrelevant.

facts.description: Queue with the lowest median handle time among valid tickets.
facts.canonical_answer: Billing is the fastest handling queue, median handle time 41 minutes
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if Billing is identified as the fastest/quickest/lowest-handle-time queue. The 41-minute median is useful but the semantic leader is the core requirement.

facts.description: Queue with the highest plan-adjusted first-response SLA rate.
facts.canonical_answer: Account is best on SLA, 5 of 6 / 83.3%
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if Account is identified as the best/highest SLA queue. It may include 83.3%, 83%, or 5/6, but the core is the correct queue and SLA context.

facts.description: Supporting context: average handle time across valid tickets, useful but not explicitly required by task.md.
facts.canonical_answer: Supporting context: average handle time is 69.8 minutes
facts.semantic_acceptance: Supporting only. Do not require it to appear; if shown, about 70 minutes / 69.8 minutes is correct.

facts.description: Concise insight based on computed queue results.
expected_value: Account leads SLA performance while Billing handles fastest.
Billing is fastest, but Account has the strongest SLA compliance.
Technical work is slower while Account leads SLA.
facts.canonical_answer: Takeaway should point to the computed support focus, e.g. Account leads SLA while Billing handles fastest
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept any concise operational takeaway grounded in the computed queue results. It need not match the sample wording.

facts.description: Do not use invalid rows, draft/forecast/subtotal records, open/cancelled tickets, internal/test requesters, or old-month rows as final results.
facts.canonical_answer: Do not use invalid support rows as final results
facts.semantic_acceptance: Strictly reject or cap if open/cancelled/internal/test/draft/forecast/subtotal/old-month rows or all-export row counts are treated as final customer-ticket results. Mentions that such rows were excluded are allowed.

calculation_notes: Use only rows with opened_date in June 2026, status=resolved, record_type=customer_ticket, and requester_type=external. Exclude record types forecast, subtotal, draft_snapshot, internal_test, plus cancelled/open statuses and old-month rows. Join plan_code to plan_sla.json and count an SLA hit when first_response_min <= first_response_sla_min. Valid ticket count=18; SLA hits=13, so first-response SLA rate=72.2%. Handle minutes sum to 1,256, so average handle time=69.8 min. Queue median handle times are Billing=41.0 min, Account=56.5 min, Technical=107.5 min. Queue SLA rates are Account=5/6=83.3%, Billing=4/6=66.7%, Technical=4/6=66.7%.
